user_message: Laura Anderson at Lucerne Publishing just sent this. Help me prepare the forecast.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Laura Anderson <laura.anderson@lucernepubli.com>
To: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-15 13:17 EST
Subject: Re: migration architecture Discussion - Next Steps

There's been a change in our timeline for migration architecture. Can we discuss the implications on the overall project plan?

---
From: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
To: Laura Anderson <laura.anderson@lucernepubli.com>
Date: 2024-01-26 11:26 EST
Subject: URGENT: migration architecture Issue at Lucerne Publishing

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with migration architecture. What are the next steps from your side?

signals.detail: Signal detected for Lucerne Publishing: the existing infrastructure won't support the target workload without upgrades

handoffs.condition: contact and opportunity records need updating

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF38-A trap (Defaults to warm-handoff without champion willingness signal); produces complete Stakeholder map delta, opportunity-at-risk assessment, re-engagement plan, contact update proposals,
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF38-A failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF38-A failure mode (Defaults to warm-handoff without champion willingness signal)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct migration architecture decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF38-A -- Defaults to warm-handoff without champion willingness signal
